purpose: Test whether a task-specific public procedural guide changes artifact quality on two non-ceiling synthetic source-task clusters, while separating guide-independent from guide-shared rubric observations.

charter_decision_filter.artifact: Hash-frozen two-cluster task/source/guide/rubric packages and an eight-attempt prospective schedule.
charter_decision_filter.uncertainty: Whether the vendor ceiling was task-specific and whether any package contrast survives a rubric not derived from the guide.
charter_decision_filter.duplication_scope: Extends rather than rewrites the completed one-task vendor matrix; LH evidence synthesis and vendor persistent state are bounded structural contrasts, not benchmark scope.
charter_decision_filter.useful_completion: Calibration discriminates valid, minimally wrong, shortcut, and invalid cases before calls; all eight scheduled attempts are retained once and replayed under both rubrics with claim ceilings intact.

randomization.algorithm: Python random.Random(seed).shuffle over two attempts for each cluster x Skill condition; attempt IDs are SHA-256-derived opaque labels independent of outcomes.
randomization.allocation_concealment: Condition mapping remains in this private protocol and outside every agent namespace.
randomization.blinding_limit: Rubrics score condition-blind identical bytes, but all instruments are builder-authored; file-access independence is not blinded human independence.

policies.outcome_tuning: No task, source, guide, rubric, grader, eligibility, denominator, stopping, or estimand change after calibration begins; version a future study instead.
policies.execution: Frozen order unless concurrent arms are launched as a predeclared adjacent pair; retain actual timestamps/order.
policies.stopping: Before calls, abort the whole v1 execution if any planted calibration class fails, any zero-call isolation/private-input canary fails, component hashes drift, or provider-reported included USD 0.00 is unavailable. After calls begin, attempt every remaining scheduled ID unless spend or safety gates require stopping; retain failed and unstarted rows and never substitute IDs.
policies.eligibility: Substantive rubric score requires passing explicit and in-trial isolation canaries, zero launcher exit, required artifacts, usage record, unchanged inputs/protected state, and both rubric records on identical bytes.
policies.invalid_missing: All eight IDs remain in availability and declared denominators. Invalid, missing, service-failed, and incomplete attempts are retained and excluded only from substantive score means; report reasons and counts by cluster/condition.
policies.cost: Execute only when the authenticated provider reports cost_status included and estimated_cost_usd 0.0. Each call must retain provider usage; missing or nonzero cost fails closed and stops later calls.
policies.attempt_dependence: Attempts are repeated executions nested in two purposively selected source-task clusters, not eight independent task samples.

lh.construct: evidence reconciliation and bounded program decision
lh.public_basis: All artifact, citation, reconciliation, threshold, experiment, stop, and change-evidence obligations are stated in lh/public-task.md.

vendor.construct: authority/temporal supersession, state delta, and bounded action in a persistent workspace
vendor.public_basis: All applicability, supersession, artifact, action, transition, and integrity obligations are stated in vendor/public-task.md.

firewalls.independent_construction: Each independent-construction manifest permits only its public task and visible sources; guide, shared rubric, calibration, graders, protocol, attempts, and repository are denied. A zero-call canary must verify the boundary before calibration.
firewalls.grader_view: For a cluster, both rubrics receive identical retained output bytes, public task/sources, environment report, and input-integrity record. Condition labels and guides are not inputs during criterion evaluation.
firewalls.lineage: Independent rubrics declare public-task/source-only lineage. Shared rubrics explicitly declare guide lineage. Neither is expert-authored or professionally validated.

requirements: Both rubrics must score the positive case above the minimally wrong and shortcut cases.
Both rubrics must fail or abstain on the invalid case for the declared reason.
Every criterion must map to an explicit public-task consequence; no guide-only obligation may affect the independent score.
At least one documented alternative valid path per cluster must receive the same pass classification as the canonical positive path.
No calibration case or result may be changed after any model output is observed.
calibration_gate.failure_action: Abort v1 before model calls; retain calibration failure and version any redesigned task or instrument as a new study.

estimands.definition: For each cluster, mean public_skill minus no_skill score under the independent rubric among eligible attempts; exact denominators retained.
For each cluster, mean public_skill minus no_skill score under the guide-shared rubric among eligible attempts; exact denominators retained.
For every eligible output, shared minus independent score on identical bytes, summarized within cluster.
Within each cluster, shared-rubric Skill contrast minus independent-rubric Skill contrast.
Report the two exact cluster contrasts side by side; do not pool or treat a two-cluster difference as a population interaction.

uncertainty.attempt_level: Report all rows, means, ranges, and sample SD only when at least two eligible attempts occupy a cell; no powered effect or inferential p-value.
uncertainty.cluster_level: Two purposively selected clusters do not estimate a domain population. Report heterogeneity descriptively and never treat attempts as independent task samples.
uncertainty.missingness: Availability, valid-environment, artifact-complete, and rubric-eligible denominators remain separate by cluster and condition.
